title: Calling on elected officials and civil society leaders to counter antisemitism and educate the public on the contributions of the Jewish American community.
plain_summary: This resolution recognizes Jewish American Heritage Month and celebrates the many ways Jewish Americans have helped make America better through their inventions and achievements. The resolution asks elected leaders, religious leaders, and community leaders to speak out against antisemitism and teach people about the important contributions Jewish Americans have made to our country. This became more important after antisemitic incidents increased following the Hamas attacks on Israel in October 2023. The goal is to fight hatred against Jewish people and help everyone learn about how Jewish Americans have helped build and improve America.
last_action_text: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
policy_area: Civil Rights and Liberties, Minority Issues

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: Jewish American Heritage Month started in nineteen eighty when Congress asked the President to create a special week in April to honor Jewish heritage. President Carter made this official and talked about how Jewish people have contributed greatly to American culture and history. Since two thousand six, every President has declared a full month to celebrate Jewish Americans and encourage all Americans to learn about Jewish contributions to our country's history. After the terrorist group Hamas attacked Israel on October seventh, two thousand twenty three, antisemitism in America reached record high levels. People are targeting Jews and those they think are Jewish. According to surveys, seventy seven percent of American Jews feel less safe in America because of these attacks. Almost seventy percent of Jewish adults say they have experienced antisemitism online including on social media. Eighty three percent of Jewish college students have experienced or seen antisemitism since the October attacks. Education about Jewish American contributions is one of the best ways to fight antisemitism and hate. Many Jewish Americans have invented things that make our daily lives better. Irving Naxon invented the slow cooker using ideas from Jewish cooking in Europe. Sylvan Goldman invented the shopping cart. Evelyn Berezin created the first real word processing computer and automated airline booking system. Ralph Baer invented the Brown Box which was the first video game system for multiple players and games. Ruth Handler started the toy company Mattel and invented the Barbie doll. Emile Berliner invented record players and microphones used in the first Bell telephones. Edwin Land invented the Polaroid instant camera. Hedy Lamarr invented frequency hopping technology that made possible wireless communications like Wi-Fi, GPS, and Bluetooth. To fight the rise of antisemitism, we need to increase awareness of how Jewish Americans have helped make America successful.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives calls on elected officials, religious leaders, and community leaders to speak out against and fight all acts of antisemitism. The House calls on the President's administration and state and local leaders to teach the public about the contributions of Jewish Americans and to lift up Jewish stories and voices. The House takes all possible steps to make sure American Jews are safe, secure, and treated with dignity in all parts of their lives including at work, on college campuses, in synagogues, and at home.
